bonuses.leadin: Bruce Nauman used this medium to create seven-foot-high signs alternating the seven vices and seven virtues in his 1988 piece, Vices and Virtues. For 10 points each:
bonuses.leadin_sanitized: Bruce Nauman used this medium to create seven-foot-high signs alternating the seven vices and seven virtues in his 1988 piece, Vices and Virtues. For 10 points each:
parts: Name these devices which use electricity and plasma to create vibrant colors.
Nam June Paik used neon lights to create outlines of the contiguous United States in this installation, which took its name from a term Paik coined in 1974 to describe the future of communication.

This artist used both neon and LEDs for the piece Protect Me From What I Want, a massive sign mounted in Times Square in 1982. A series by this artist displays aphorisms on various media, such as benches, light displays, and wooden plaques.

bonuses.leadin: Along with its northern neighbor, this country is home to a musical genre in which a solo vocalist sings a story while a second performer beats a drum, called pansori. For 10 points each:
bonuses.leadin_sanitized: Along with its northern neighbor, this country is home to a musical genre in which a solo vocalist sings a story while a second performer beats a drum, called pansori. For 10 points each:
parts: Name this Asian country home to a pioneer of video art who had Charlotte Moorman draw her bow across three stacked television screens for his TV Cello. This country produced Nam June Paik and director Park Chan-Wook.

This video installation by Paik, named for a phrase he coined, consists of a large map of the United States with state borders formed by neon lights. Each state is filled in with a set of television screens showing a unique feed.

This artist’s foray into video art includes his Hand Catching Lead, a film showing his hand repeatedly trying to grab at a falling piece of lead. This sculptor used very large sheets of metal for The Matter of Time and Tilted Arc.

answers_sanitized: Electronic Superhighway: Continental U.S., Alaska, Hawaii
